kaulika (von kula)
1〉 adj. vom den Eltern herkommend, ererbt  ŚKDR.   WILS. 
— 2〉 m.
a〉 Weber  ŚABDAM. _im_ ŚKDR.   PAÑCAT. 35,15. fgg.  42,25 . 132,23. fgg.  249,22. fgg.  varjayetkaulikākāraṃ mitraṃ prajñataro naraḥ . ātmanaḥ saṃmukhaṃ nityaṃ ya ākarṣati lolupaḥ .. IV, 12 .
— b〉 Ketzer  TRIK. 2,7,28 .
— c〉 a follower of the left hand Śākta ritual  WILS.  Vgl. kaula.

results.rendered_html:
kaulika a. (kī f.) [kulādāgataḥ
(pb) ṭhak]
1 Belonging to a family.
2 Customary in a family, ancestral.
— kaḥ
1 A weaver; kauliko viṣṇurūpeṇa rājakanyāṃ niṣevate  Pt. 1. 202. 
2 An impostor, a heretic.
3 A follower of the left hand Śākta ritual.
